title: THE NUCLEOLUS OF THE BANKRUPTCY PROBLEM BY HYDRAULIC RATIONING
abstract: In this note, we give a straightforward and elementary proof of a theorem by Aumann and Maschler stating that in the well-known bankruptcy problem, the so-called CG-consistent solution described by the Talmud represents the nucleolus of the corresponding coalitional game. The proof nicely fits into the hydraulic rationing framework proposed by Kaminski. We point out further interesting properties in connection with this framework.
summary_en: The Talmud's solution to the bankruptcy problem — known to economists as CG-consistent — has a deep game-theoretic justification. Fleiner and Sziklai give a clean, elementary proof of Aumann and Maschler's theorem stating that this CG-consistent solution represents the nucleolus of the corresponding coalitional game. The proof fits naturally into Kaminski's hydraulic-rationing framework. The authors highlight further interesting properties of the rationing framework that follow from this perspective. A short technical contribution that makes the Aumann-Maschler result accessible to researchers without deep coalitional-game-theory background.
data_used: Theoretical paper; no empirical data. Mathematical proof in the framework of cooperative game theory and hydraulic rationing.
policy_relevance: Foundational for Hungarian researchers working on bankruptcy and rationing problems — debt restructuring, financial crisis resolution, rationing of healthcare or social benefits. The CG-consistent solution and its nucleolus interpretation provide a principled benchmark for 'fair' rationing rules. Indirect relevance to MNB resolution-procedure design and to Pénzügyminisztérium debt-restructuring policy.
